response:
The fare $\eta$ depends on the random driving distance $\xi$ through a piecewise function where the base cost is ten yuan for distances up to four kilometers. When $\xi$ exceeds four kilometers the additional charge becomes two yuan per rounded kilometer of excess distance. This relationship can be expressed as $\eta = 10 + 2 \times \lceil \xi - 4 \rceil$ for any $\xi$ greater than four. The spring season often brings more travelers to the airport which increases the demand for such taxi services.

To find the maximum stopping time we first calculate the excess distance that generated the thirty-eight yuan fare. Since the base fare is ten yuan the extra twenty-eight yuan corresponds to fourteen rounded kilometers of excess travel. The actual physical distance was fifteen kilometers so the equivalent distance from stopping time must be at least one kilometer to reach the rounded total. One kilometer of equivalent distance equals five minutes of stopping time according to the city rules. Therefore the taxi stopped for at most five minutes during the trip to the hotel. The spring weather might have caused traffic delays that required this brief stop.
